title: message_create: client_id_str intermediate inconsistent with other callsites
description: In message_create, resolve_client_id() returns Cow<str> and an explicit let client_id_str: &str = &client_id is created because client_id is used in both the serde_json::json! macro and not_created.get(). Other callsites (chat.rs, space.rs, space_ban.rs, space_invite.rs, custom_emoji.rs) use the Cow<str> directly in json! because they only need it once. The inconsistency is technically correct but a contributor who copies message.rs will not understand why the intermediate exists; a contributor who copies chat.rs into a function that needs client_id twice will get a borrow conflict. Add a comment: // used twice: json! key and not_created.get() lookup — borrow from Cow to avoid double-move
